title: while exiting `podman exec` pinging error occurs
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

when using `podman exec ` (whatever command you use thereafter) pinging error occurs.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. use podman exec 
> $ podman exec [container name] id


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received
```
[conmon:d]: exec with attach is waiting for start message from parent
[conmon:d]: exec with attach got start message from parent
DEBU[0000] Container 825dd9687d77f3e30cf74ce6e681cbcebd2798c7ea9e2d34843ccff6505f8ca1 exec session 3547ad76c81e3c55fea74298da4a3ee2162fcf69911d2489aea460cee90dd02a completed with exit code 0 
DEBU[0000] Checking status of container 825dd9687d77f3e30cf74ce6e681cbcebd2798c7ea9e2d34843ccff6505f8ca1 exec session 3547ad76c81e3c55fea74298da4a3ee2162fcf69911d2489aea460cee90dd02a 
INFO[0000] Removing container 825dd9687d77f3e30cf74ce6e681cbcebd2798c7ea9e2d34843ccff6505f8ca1 exec session 3547ad76c81e3c55fea74298da4a3ee2162fcf69911d2489aea460cee90dd02a 
DEBU[0000] Successfully removed container 825dd9687d77f3e30cf74ce6e681cbcebd2798c7ea9e2d34843ccff6505f8ca1 exec session 3547ad76c81e3c55fea74298da4a3ee2162fcf69911d2489aea460cee90dd02a 
Error: pinging container 825dd9687d77f3e30cf74ce6e681cbcebd2798c7ea9e2d34843ccff6505f8ca1 exec session 3547ad76c81e3c55fea74298da4a3ee2162fcf69911d2489aea460cee90dd02a PID 0 with signal 0: permission denied
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines                        
INFO[0000] Received shutdown.Stop(), terminating!        PID=2130307
```

I updated podman version into 5.8.3 but looks same.

```
DEBU[0000] Successfully removed container 825dd9687d77f3e30cf74ce6e681cbcebd2798c7ea9e2d34843ccff6505f8ca1 exec session 3547ad76c81e3c55fea74298da4a3ee2162fcf69911d2489aea460cee90dd02a 
Error:  getting the process status for pid : permission denied
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines                        
INFO[0000] Received shutdown.Stop(), terminating!        PID=2130307
```

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

no permission denied message

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

I checked RedHat SR and they said both the Exec UpdateStatus, and the Exec StopContainer are identified as structures that can expose me to the same problem in certain situations. Later versions reflected some improvements, and the underlying structural improvements were made in the higher version (5.6.0) but occuring same.

we have some situation to use exec from gitlab runnner to podman for reload some middleware software.

if error occurs, pipeline might be failed even if the success.

comments.body:
issues with rhel should be reported to the distribution through the normal mechanisms.  upstream is focused on developing the latest and future versions of podman.

i cant remember the details, but pinging used to be a somewhat privileged command.  does it work as a privileged user?  you also dont provide the command you are running and the image.  that might also help.

> issues with rhel should be reported to the distribution through the normal mechanisms. upstream is focused on developing the latest and future versions of podman.
> 
> i cant remember the details, but pinging used to be a somewhat privileged command. does it work as a privileged user? you also dont provide the command you are running and the image. that might also help.

Do all parts of RHEL OS environment need to contact redhat?
For Podman, I'm using a regular distribution. For the current 5.8.3, I got it directly from the rpm repository and installed it.

The exact command and image are not specified because they occur the same way when all exec commands are used. Currently, images used in that environment are nginx, nginx-unprivatized, and haproxy. 
The ping failure phenomenon is also found in the privileged podman environment. Logically, it seems that the same phenomenon is seen regardless of the authority because we are pinging for cases that have already ended the process.

> Do all parts of RHEL OS environment need to contact redhat?

Hi @CooolRyan ... to be clear, I work for RH, so I look at this way.  If you are a paying customer for RHEL and you have a legit bug, you likely want that fixed in a version you consume. This happens more smoothly if you file that bug with RH, because it glues together all the process *you and we* need to deliver that fix.

If you are only interested in fixing this upstream in the main branch version (aka "the next version of podman"), then replicating on the latest Podman version and reporting here is totally kosher.

With the former, you will likely get support help quicker to establish if this is a bug, etc. so that might also be valuable to you.

First of all, we found that the security software used inside the company blocks the kill(0,0) process when calling the kill(0,0) process during the end of the exec session. 

`./podman-new-exec.trace.3911389:1:10:46:15.146406 kill(0, 0)              = -1 EACCES (Permission denied)`

We would like to end the issue because it is an internal matter. 

When I looked at the security software log, the 5.8.3 log standard `Error: getting the process status for pid 0: permission denied` was shown as a privilege issue while reading the /proc sub-path. It was difficult to guess from the error log only. lol
